software-engineer: Walk me through a recent project you built end-to-end. What trade-offs did you make?
Explain the difference between a process and a thread. When would you choose each?
How would you design a URL shortener? Talk about data model, scale, and failure modes.
Describe a performance issue you debugged. What metrics did you use and what was the root cause?
What is your approach to writing maintainable code in a large codebase?
web-developer: What happens when you type a URL in browser and press Enter?
How do you manage state in a React application and when do you choose each approach?
Explain CORS and how you would debug a CORS issue.
How would you design an authentication flow for a SPA with a backend API?
Tell me about a time you improved page performance. What did you change?
ui-ux-designer: Walk me through your design process from discovery to handoff.
How do you validate design decisions with users and stakeholders?
Explain a design system you've contributed to. What components/tokens did it include?
How do you handle conflicting feedback from different stakeholders?
Describe a UX problem you identified using data. What did you change?

interview-questions: How do you approach offline-first behavior in a mobile app?
Explain your strategy for handling push notifications end-to-end.
How do you optimize app startup time and reduce perceived latency?
Describe how you handle different screen sizes, accessibility, and platform conventions.
Tell me about a tricky bug you solved on mobile. How did you reproduce it?

video-resources.title: Mobile App Development Introduction
video-resources.description: Complete guide to mobile app development fundamentals

video-resources.title: Advanced Mobile Development Techniques
video-resources.description: Professional mobile app development best practices

text-resources.title: What is Mobile App Development Process

content.introduction: Mobile app development is about designing and building applications specifically for mobile devices. Unlike desktop software, mobile apps are tailored to work on smaller screens, touch-based interfaces, and often limited processing power. Developers use programming languages like Swift for Apple's iOS platform or Kotlin/Java for Android devices.
key_areas: User Experience (UX) and User Interface (UI) Design: Creating an app that's easy to use and visually appealing.
Backend Development: Building the server-side systems that power the app, like databases or cloud services.
Quality Assurance (QA): Testing the app to ensure it works smoothly without bugs.

development_process.description: Brainstorming phase where developers identify unique value proposition, target audience, and key features.
Defining scope, market research, project plan, and creating user stories and prototypes.
Creating UI/UX design, choosing colors, fonts, icons, and building interactive mockups.
Frontend development (interface) and backend development (server-side systems) using Swift/Kotlin/Java.
Functionality testing, usability testing, performance testing, and bug fixing.
Submitting to app stores, preparing listings, and launching the app.
Ongoing bug fixes, updates, and ensuring compatibility with new devices and OS updates.

cost_factors: Complexity: Simple apps cost less than complex ones
Design: Custom designs are more expensive
Platform: iOS and Android development costs more
Team Experience and Location: Varies by region

devops-engineer: Explain a CI/CD pipeline you designed. What were the key stages and why?
How do you monitor a production system and define useful alerts?
What is Infrastructure as Code and how do you manage drift?
Walk through how you would handle a production incident (on-call scenario).
How do you secure secrets across environments and deployments?
data-scientist: Describe a machine learning project you shipped. How did you measure success?
How do you handle missing data and outliers during preprocessing?
Explain overfitting and techniques you use to prevent it.
How do you communicate results to non-technical stakeholders?
Tell me about a time a model performed poorly in production. What did you do?
product-manager: How do you decide what to build next when you have competing priorities?
Describe how you write user stories and define acceptance criteria.
How do you measure product success and choose metrics that matter?
Tell me about a product decision you reversed after learning new information.
How do you align engineering, design, and business stakeholders?
marketing-specialist: How do you plan and evaluate a marketing campaign end-to-end?
What metrics do you track for performance marketing and why?
How do you define and test messaging and positioning?
Describe a time you improved conversion rate. What experiments did you run?
How do you segment audiences and personalize communication?
hr-recruiter: Walk me through how you screen candidates for both skills and culture add.
How do you structure behavioral interviews to reduce bias?
Describe how you work with hiring managers to define a strong role profile.
How do you handle a candidate who negotiates aggressively?
Tell me about a difficult hiring decision you had to make and how you made it.

behavioral-upskilling: Describe a time you received critical feedback. What did you do next?
Tell me about a conflict with a teammate and how you resolved it.
How do you handle working with someone who has a very different style than you?
Describe a time you made a mistake at work. How did you communicate it?
How do you prioritize tasks when everything feels urgent?
presentation-skills: Explain a complex topic in 60 seconds to a non-technical audience.
How do you structure a presentation to keep an audience engaged?
What techniques do you use to manage nervousness on stage?
How do you handle tough questions during Q&A?
Describe how you would improve a slide deck that is too text-heavy.
communication-boost: What does active listening mean to you and how do you practice it?
Describe a time you had to communicate bad news. How did you do it?
How do you adapt your communication style for different stakeholders?
Give an example of how you clarified ambiguity in requirements.
How do you make sure your message is understood (not just delivered)?
leadership-skills: Describe a time you influenced without authority.
How do you motivate a team when morale is low?
What is your approach to delegation and accountability?
Tell me about a time you had to make a decision with incomplete information.
How do you handle underperformance on your team?
negotiation-skills: Describe a negotiation you led. What was your strategy and outcome?
How do you define your BATNA and use it in negotiation?
How do you handle a counterpart who uses pressure tactics?
Walk me through how you negotiate scope or timelines with stakeholders.
What does a win-win outcome look like in practice?
time-management: How do you plan your week to balance deep work and meetings?
What system do you use to track tasks and priorities?
Tell me about a time you missed a deadline. What did you learn?
How do you avoid context switching and regain focus quickly?
How do you estimate work when requirements are uncertain?
stress-management: What are your early signs of stress and how do you respond?
Describe a high-pressure situation and how you stayed effective.
How do you set boundaries to prevent burnout?
What routines help you recover after intense work periods?
How do you approach problems when you feel overwhelmed?
networking-skills: How do you introduce yourself in a professional setting in 30 seconds?
Describe how you maintain relationships over time, not just when you need something.
How do you approach reaching out to someone you admire professionally?
Tell me about a time networking helped you solve a problem or find an opportunity.
How do you provide value to your network proactively?
critical-thinking: Describe how you break down a vague problem into actionable steps.
Tell me about a time you challenged an assumption and it changed the outcome.
How do you evaluate trade-offs when there is no perfect solution?
Walk through how you would analyze a new idea before committing resources.
How do you separate signal from noise when reviewing information quickly?
